scenes.text: In the first scene of our Docker lecture, we'll introduce the concept of containerization. We'll discuss how Docker allows developers to package applications and their dependencies into a standardized unit for software development. This makes deployment processes much simpler and more efficient. The key components include images, containers, the Docker engine, and Docker Hub. These elements work together to create an ecosystem that supports seamless application management.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Developers] --> B(Docker Images); B --> C(Docker Containers); C --> D[Applications]; B --> E[Docker Hub]; F[Docker Engine] --> C

scenes.text: The second scene focuses on the practical usage of Docker. We'll explore how developers can use Docker commands to manage and deploy containers. This includes building Docker images using Dockerfile, running containers using 'docker run', and managing container lifecycle effectively. The mastery of these commands allows for robust and reliable software deployment across various environments.
scenes.image_prompt: sequenceDiagram; participant Dev as Developer; participant CLI as Command Line; participant Docker as Docker System; Dev->>CLI: Execute docker build; CLI->>Docker: Build image; Dev->>CLI: Execute docker run; CLI->>Docker: Start container
